name: gene-database
description: Query NCBI Gene via E-utilities/Datasets API. Search by symbol/ID, retrieve gene info (RefSeqs, GO, locations, phenotypes), batch lookups, for gene annotation and functional analysis.
capabilities: Search NCBI Gene by symbol, name, or ID across species
Retrieve gene sequences, RefSeqs, and metadata via official APIs
Fetch Gene Ontology annotations and phenotype associations
Process batch gene lookups with rate limiting and error handling
Output data in JSON, XML, or text formats
Query by chromosomal location, disease, or pathway
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: This is a legitimate bioinformatics tool for querying NCBI Gene database. All 374 static findings are false positives: markdown backticks in documentation are code formatting (not shell execution), API keys are user-provided CLI arguments (not hardcoded secrets), hardcoded URLs are official NCBI API endpoints, and the network+credential+file pattern is standard API client behavior. No data exfiltration or malicious patterns detected.
valueStatement: Access comprehensive gene data from NCBI including sequences, annotations, and functional information. Search by gene symbol, ID, or biological context to retrieve RefSeqs, Gene Ontology, chromosomal locations, and phenotypes for bioinformatics research.
limitations: Rate limited to 3 requests per second without API key
Only accesses NCBI Gene database, not other genomic resources
No built-in data analysis or visualization capabilities
Requires internet connection to access NCBI APIs

useCases.title: Gene annotation workflows
useCases.description: Retrieve comprehensive gene data including sequences, GO terms, and phenotypes for functional analysis.

useCases.title: Batch gene data collection
useCases.description: Process gene lists efficiently with rate limiting for large-scale genomic studies.

useCases.title: Gene information lookup
useCases.description: Query gene symbols and retrieve detailed information for educational purposes.

promptTemplates.title: Find a gene symbol
promptTemplates.prompt: Find information about the {gene_symbol} gene in {organism} including its chromosomal location and function.

promptTemplates.title: Get sequence data
promptTemplates.prompt: Retrieve RefSeq accession numbers and gene sequence data for {gene_id} in JSON format.

promptTemplates.title: Batch gene lookup
promptTemplates.prompt: Look up gene information for the following genes from {organism}: {gene_list}. Return the results as JSON.

promptTemplates.title: Gene function search
promptTemplates.prompt: Find genes associated with {biological_process} in {organism} using Gene Ontology terms.

bestPractices: Include the organism in your query to avoid ambiguous results from gene symbols that exist in multiple species
Use Gene IDs instead of symbols when precision is required, as gene symbols can change over time
Include your NCBI API key for faster batch processing (up to 10 requests per second)
antiPatterns: Do not make individual requests in a loop without rate limiting, as this will trigger NCBI rate limits
Do not rely solely on gene symbols for permanent storage, as official nomenclature can change
Do not assume all gene symbols are unique within an organism without verifying

faq.question: How do I get an NCBI API key?
faq.answer: Register for a free API key at https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/account to increase rate limits from 3 to 10 requests per second.

faq.question: What gene formats are supported?
faq.answer: You can search by official gene symbol (BRCA1), Gene ID (672), or use complex queries with GO terms and disease keywords.

faq.question: Can I retrieve protein sequences?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill retrieves RefSeq accessions for transcripts and proteins associated with the gene.

faq.question: How many genes can I query at once?
faq.answer: The batch script supports hundreds of genes with automatic rate limiting. ESummary accepts up to 500 IDs per request.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Data can be returned as JSON for programmatic use, XML for detailed metadata, or human-readable text summaries.

faq.question: Does this skill store any data?
faq.answer: No, this skill only queries NCBI APIs in real-time. All data is returned directly to the user or written to a user-specified output file.
